escalatedRisks.risk: 🔴🔴 Japan WF6 (tungsten hexafluoride) permanent production halt July 1, 2026 — structural semiconductor supply chain crisis
escalatedRisks.status: CRITICAL NEW - June 10, 2026 (09:46 second pass). Showa Denko Kanto + Central Glass (~25% global WF6 capacity) notified Samsung, SK Hynix, TSMC that WF6 stocks last only through June; production ceases permanently July 1. Cause: China high-purity tungsten powder exports to Japan stopped since Jan 2026 (60-70% of WF6 cost, +325% YoY price surge). WF6 is irreplaceable for CVD tungsten deposition in DRAM, 3D NAND, and logic chip manufacturing. New supplier qualification takes 12-18 months. This could surpass HBM wafer competition as the single largest supply constraint in semiconductor history.

Mouser_QLC_E1L.note: 4 units confirmed recovery, false alarm cleared

WF6_Japan.note: Permanent halt July 1, 2026. ~25% global capacity. Showa Denko Kanto + Central Glass. 12-18 month requalification cycle.
